top_signals.title: Ukraine’s drone playbook is wreaking havoc in Russia — and upending where NATO wants to invest
top_signals.summary: Ukraine’s deep drone strikes on Russian refineries are reshaping the war and pushing NATO toward a $40 billion counter-drone plan.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: India ramps up missile sales in Indo-Pacific as China’s assertiveness turns neighbors wary
top_signals.summary: India has signed a third pact for the supply of missiles in the Indo-Pacific region as countries in the region grow wary of China's growing assertion.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Anthropic appoints former Fed Chair Ben Bernanke to its independent trust
top_signals.summary: Members of the trust advise Anthropic leadership and do not hold any equity in the company.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Frank Elderson: The green transition – benefits and barriers
Corporate report: UK Government Investments Annual Report and Accounts 2025-26
top_signals.summary: This annual report and accounts provides an overview of UKGI’s activities during the 2025-26 financial year.

top_signals.title: Permian natural gas production increased faster than crude oil
top_signals.summary: The Permian region's marketed natural gas production grew from 17.2 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) in 2021 to 27.6 Bcf/d in 2025, a 60% increase, according to data from our latest Short Term Energy Outlook. Over the same period, crude oil production grew by 39%, going from 4.7 million barrels per day (b/d) to 6.6 million b/d. The higher growth in natural gas production is the result of increasing gas-oil ratios (GOR).
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Climate models become more precise even as political attacks sharpen
top_signals.summary: Climate researchers have operated under political duress for decades, but improved resources and understanding in recent years have made findings more useful to policymakers.
